theorem: contrapose_demo (P Q : Prop) [Decidable P] [Decidable Q] (h : P → Q) : ¬Q → ¬P

steps.label: Assume ¬Q
steps.explanation: We're proving the contrapositive. Start by assuming Q is false.

strategy.trigger: Goal is an implication — use direct proof (intro)

steps.label: Assume P (to derive contradiction)
steps.explanation: ¬P means P → False. So we assume P and need to derive False. Now we have hp : P and h : P → Q, so we can get Q. But we also have hnq : ¬Q...

strategy.rule: To prove ¬A, assume A and derive False

steps.label: Chain the contradiction
steps.explanation: h hp : Q (applying the implication to our assumption). hnq (h hp) : False (applying ¬Q to Q). The chain: P → Q → False. Contradiction!
